title: 10 best herbal tinctures for fatigue
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for fatigue are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate physical and mental exhaustion. These remedies work by increasing energy levels, improving mood, and enhancing cognitive function. The best herbal tinctures for fatigue include Rhodiola rosea, Schisandra chinensis, Ginkgo biloba, Eleutherococcus senticosus, Panax ginseng, Valeriana officinalis, Withania somnifera, Panax quinquefolius, Lavandula angustifolia, and Centella asiatica. If left untreated, fatigue can severely impact daily life, leading to decreased productivity, strained relationships, and a diminished quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains adaptogenic compounds that help to regulate the body's energy levels. The active ingredients in ginseng, such as ginsenosides and saponins, work to improve mental clarity and focus, reducing feelings of exhaustion and lethargy. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to increase oxygenation of the body's cells, allowing for more efficient energy production and reduced fatigue. By promoting overall physical and mental well-being, ginseng tinctures can help individuals overcome persistent feelings of tiredness and regain their natural vitality.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng tincture helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can contribute to fatigue, by promoting resilience and reducing feelings of exhaustion.
Antioxidant properties: Ginseng tincture contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic fatigue and weakness.

Cognitive-enhancing properties: Ginseng tincture has been shown to improve cognitive function, including memory, attention, and mental clarity, which can help alleviate feelings of fatigue and mental fogginess.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins help alleviate fatigue by enhancing mitochondrial function and improving energy production in cells, thereby increasing physical endurance and reducing mental exhaustion.

Glycosides: Ginseng glycosides, particularly ginsenoside Rb1, have been shown to reduce fatigue by modulating the body's response to stress and improving the balance of neurotransmitters in the brain, leading to improved mood and reduced fatigue.

Eleutherosides: These triterpene saponins, which are also found in Panax ginseng, have been demonstrated to help combat fatigue by stimulating the release of certain neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, and by increasing the body's adaptive response to stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have adaptogenic and anti-fatigue properties.
Stems: The stems are used as they contain ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing fatigue and improving mental clarity.
Leaves: The leaves are also utilized due to their ability to enhance cognitive function and reduce fatigue, although their ginsenoside content is lower compared to roots and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the panax ginseng roots in the fall season when their color has changed to a lighter shade.
Clean and dry the panax ginseng roots thoroughly in a warm place for at least seven days.
Chop the dried panax ginseng roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and weighing about 250 grams.
Steep the chopped panax ginseng roots in 1 liter of 80% ethanol for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture and discard the solids then filter it through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains adaptogenic properties that allow the body to cope with stress, promoting energy levels and mental clarity. The herb has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to combat exhaustion and weakness, as it helps to regulate cortisol levels, reducing feelings of burnout and increasing vitality. By supporting adrenal function, ashwagandha tinctures help to alleviate fatigue, allowing individuals to tackle their daily tasks with renewed vigor and enthusiasm.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha tincture helps to adapt the body to stress and promotes resilience, which can help alleviate feelings of fatigue caused by chronic stress.

Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidants help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, improving overall energy levels and reducing oxidative stress.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Ashwagandha tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can lead to improved sleep quality and reduced morning fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones, particularly withanolide A, have anti-fatigue effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby helping to alleviate fatigue.
Withaferin A: This withanolide is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce muscle fatigue and improve endurance by protecting cells from oxidative damage.

Alkaloids (particularly withanine and isowithanolide D): These compounds help to regulate the body's energy balance by improving mitochondrial function and reducing fatigue-related oxidative stress, ultimately enhancing energy levels and reducing exhaustion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, such as withanolides, responsible for the medicinal properties of the plant.
Leaves: They contain a range of withanolides and other compounds that contribute to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Although less commonly used, Withania somnifera fruits are known to contain withanolides and other phytochemicals that may help alleviate fatigue and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried withania somnifera roots and stems in the morning or afternoon.
Chop the harvested roots and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife or tool.
Combine the chopped roots and stems with 80-100 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:2 to 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden root tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to boost energy levels and alleviate feelings of exhaustion. The tincture's primary active compound, rhizome extract, has been shown to increase ATP production in the body, allowing for more efficient use of energy. Additionally, golden root's adaptogenic properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, further supporting overall energy production and reducing fatigue. As a result, golden root tinctures can provide a natural and effective solution for combating chronic fatigue.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Golden root helps to adapt to stress and increases resilience, reducing the feeling of fatigue by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting physical and mental endurance.

Antioxidant activity: Golden root's high antioxidant content helps protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, and supports overall energy production in the body.

Cognitive-enhancing properties: Golden root's ability to increase dopamine and serotonin levels in the brain helps improve mental clarity, focus, and mood, reducing feelings of fatigue and improving overall mental performance.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Helps with fatigue by enhancing the body's natural ability to resist fatigue and stress, improving mental performance and mood.
Salidroside: Contributes to reducing fatigue by acting as an antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals and protecting the body from oxidative stress.
Tyrosol: Relieves fatigue by possessing neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, promoting neural health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of rhodiola's bioactive compounds, including rosavin and salidroside, which contribute to its adaptogenic and anti-fatigue properties.
Leaves: They contain a significant amount of the bioactive compounds responsible for rhodiola's adaptogenic and stress-relieving effects, although less so than the roots.
Stems: The stems also contain bioactive compounds, although in smaller amounts compared to the roots and leaves, and are often used in combination with other parts to enhance the tincture's effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh rhodiola rosea root or 5-10 grams of dried root at dawn to capture optimal potency.
Dry the harvested rhodiola root in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Chop the dried rhodiola root into small pieces and combine it with 50% ethanol by volume in a glass container.
Store the container in a cool dark place and steep for 2-4 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to normalize cortisol levels in the body. This herb has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to feelings of exhaustion. Additionally, Brahmi is known to enhance focus and mental clarity, making it easier to tackle tasks and overcome mental fatigue. As a natural stimulant, Brahmi also increases oxygen flow to the brain, leaving users feeling revitalized and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Brahmi tincture helps adapt to stress by reducing cortisol levels, which can contribute to fatigue, and promotes resilience in the face of physical or mental exhaustion.

Nootropic effects: Brahmi contains bioactive compounds like bacosides A and B, which have been shown to enhance cognitive function, improve memory, and increase alertness, making it a natural remedy for fatigue-related brain fog.

Antioxidant properties: Brahmi's rich antioxidant content helps protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic fatigue syndrome and other fatigue-related conditions by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of glycosides that contribute to Bacopa monnieri's nootropic and adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce fatigue by enhancing cognitive function and promoting mental resilience.

Bacosterol: Bacosterol is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have antioxidant and neuroprotective properties, which help to mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to fatigue.
Monnierin: Monnierin is an alkaloid that has been found to have a stimulatory effect on the nervous system, helping to increase alertness and energy levels, and reduce fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Bacopa monnieri are widely used to make tinctures for fatigue due to their rich content of bacosides, which have been shown to enhance cognitive function and memory.
Stems: The stems of Bacopa monnieri are also used to make tinctures for fatigue due to their high concentration of bacosides, which help to improve mental clarity and reduce mental fatigue.

Roots: The roots of Bacopa monnieri are another commonly used part to make tinctures for fatigue, as they contain a high amount of bacosides and other bioactive compounds that promote mental well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated for best potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Chop 100 grams of the dried plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped plant material in 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a dark glass bottle to remove plant residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains adaptogenic properties that allow the body to cope with physical, mental, and emotional stress. This ancient herbal remedy stimulates the production of energy-boosting compounds in the brain, such as ATP and NADH, which increase alertness and vitality. Additionally, Siberian ginseng tinctures have been shown to improve oxygenation and circulation, reducing feelings of exhaustion and fatigue, allowing individuals to tackle daily tasks with renewed energy and enthusiasm.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Siberian Ginseng helps the body adapt to stress and promotes resilience, reducing fatigue caused by chronic stress and exhaustion.
Improves energy metabolism: Siberian Ginseng has been shown to increase ATP production in cells, enhancing energy levels and reducing feelings of fatigue and lethargy.

Antioxidant activity: Siberian Ginseng's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, brain fog, and decreased mental clarity.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Eleutherococcus senticosus tinctures help with fatigue by increasing the body's adaptogenic response, which enables it to cope with stress and maintain energy levels.

Stilbenes (including Eleutherosides): Stilbenes, particularly Eleutherosides, help with fatigue by modulating the body's antioxidant defenses and promoting the recovery of cells and tissues damaged by stress.

Flavonoids (including Rutin and Quercetin): Flavonoids, such as Rutin and Quercetin, help with fatigue by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which helps to increase oxygen delivery to cells and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of Eleutherococcus senticosus's bioactive compounds, making them the most commonly used part for fatigue relief.
Barks: Eleutherococcus senticosus's bark is also rich in bioactive compounds and is often used as an alternative to roots, due to its similar composition.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain some of the same bioactive compounds as the roots and barks, making them a viable option for tinctures related to fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots and stems at the end of the growing season.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently swishing it in a bowl of cool water.
Combine the cleaned plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it every few days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with fatigue because it supports the body's natural ability to relax and rejuvenate. The herb valerian root has been used for centuries to promote a good night's sleep and reduce stress, both of which are often underlying causes of fatigue. By calming the mind and soothing the body, valerian tincture can help increase energy levels and reduce feelings of exhaustion. Additionally, it can also help regulate the body's natural rhythms, leading to improved overall well-being and a sense of renewed vitality.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Valerian tincture helps to adapt to stress and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of fatigue by calming the nervous system and promoting deep sleep.

GABA-enhancing effects: Valerian root contains compounds that increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, which can alleviate fatigue caused by stress, anxiety, or insomnia.

Vasodilatory effects: Valerian tincture has been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing oxygen delivery to tissues, which can help combat physical fatigue caused by reduced blood flow.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue by enhancing the activity of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA).
Valerenol and isovalerenol: These sesquiterpenes have sedative properties, helping to reduce fatigue by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality.
Valeranone: It acts as a GABA receptor agonist, similar to valerenic acid, and also exhibits anxiolytic and sedative properties, contributing to reduced fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which is responsible for the herb's sedative and relaxing properties.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used in tinctures as they contain a smaller amount of valerenic acid than the roots, but still offer some sedative and anti-anxiety effects.

Flowers: Valerian flowers are used in some herbal remedies, particularly those aimed at promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, although their effects are generally milder than those of the roots or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the fall or early spring when they are at their peak potency for best results.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part dried valeriana officinalis roots with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain a unique combination of natural compounds that work together to enhance energy levels. The tannins in maypop help to stimulate the body's production of ATP, the energy currency used by cells, while its flavonoids increase blood flow and oxygenation to tired muscles. Additionally, maypop's adaptogenic properties allow it to regulate cortisol levels, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to fatigue. By addressing these underlying causes, maypop tinctures provide a natural boost of energy and vitality.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Maypop tincture contains adaptogenic compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, which help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the nervous system, reducing feelings of fatigue and exhaustion.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in maypop tincture help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, brain fog, and other symptoms related to oxidative stress.

Vasodilatory properties: Maypop tincture's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood circulation, increasing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and organs, which can help alleviate fatigue, headaches, and other symptoms associated with poor circulation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as flavone and kaempferol, help alleviate fatigue by acting as antioxidants and reducing oxidative stress in the body.
Herniarin: Herniarin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps combat fatigue by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation.
Isorhapontigenin: Isorhapontigenin, a flavonoid with neuroprotective properties, helps alleviate fatigue by reducing inflammation in the brain and promoting better sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to the herb's sedative and anti-fatigue properties.
Roots: The roots are a secondary choice, often used in combination with the leaves, as they contain similar compounds and may provide additional benefits.
Stems: The stems are occasionally used, offering a more subtle effect, although their use is less common due to their lower concentration of bioactive compounds compared to leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in abundance for later use.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material thoroughly to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of high proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the extraction process to occur.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate the tincture from solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains a rich source of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that help to energize and revitalize the body. The tincture's unique blend of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes works to stimulate the production of ATP, the body's primary energy molecule, while also supporting the health of the mitochondria, the powerhouses within our cells. By promoting cellular respiration and energy metabolism, maidenhair tree tincture helps to alleviate fatigue and increase vitality, leaving users feeling refreshed and invigorated.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Stress-Relieving Properties: Maidenhair Tree tincture contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids, which help the body adapt to stress and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of fatigue.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in Maidenhair Tree tincture help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, improving overall energy levels.

Cognitive-Enhancing and Neuroprotective Effects: The bioactive compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture have been shown to improve cognitive function, including memory and concentration, helping to combat mental fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): This terpenoid has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate fatigue by increasing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to tired cells.

Ginkgolides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fatigue. They also have antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage, which can also help alleviate fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba for tinctures to alleviate fatigue due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Seeds: The seeds are also used in tinctures to combat fatigue, as they are rich in bilobalide, a compound with neuroprotective properties.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are sometimes used in tinctures for fatigue relief, possibly due to their potential antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and dried.
Chop 100 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca tinctures helps with fatigue because it's a natural adaptogen that nourishes the adrenal glands, enabling them to better cope with stress. The rich concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids in maca root stimulates energy production, increasing ATP levels and oxygenation of cells. Additionally, maca tinctures can help regulate hormones, including cortisol, which plays a crucial role in energy metabolism. By addressing hormonal imbalances and supporting adrenal function, maca tinctures provide a natural boost to combat fatigue and improve overall vitality.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Maca tincture helps to adapt the body's response to stress, reducing feelings of fatigue and increasing energy levels by normalizing cortisol and adrenaline levels.

Antioxidant activity: Maca tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to fatigue, inflammation, and oxidative stress, promoting overall cellular health and well-being.

Vitality-boosting saponins: Maca tincture contains saponins, which are believed to help increase oxygenation of the blood, reduce fatigue, and improve exercise performance by enhancing cardiovascular function.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Lepidium meyenii tinctures, such as macamides and macaridines, help alleviate fatigue by stimulating the body's energy production and reducing oxidative stress.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like ferulic acid and caffeic acid in Maca tinctures help combat fatigue by neutralizing free radicals and improving the body's antioxidant defenses, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting energy balance.

Sterols: Sterols, particularly brassicasterol and campesterol, in Lepidium meyenii tinctures help alleviate fatigue by regulating hormone balance, improving adrenal function, and enhancing the body's overall energy metabolism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Maca contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including glucosinolates, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
Seeds: Maca seeds are rich in nutrients and contain a higher concentration of essential fatty acids, proteins, and other beneficial compounds compared to its roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest lepidium meyenii roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Clean the harvested material by gently rinsing with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned lepidium meyenii into small pieces with a sharp knife to increase the surface area.
Combine the chopped lepidium meyenii with 80 proof ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain bioactive compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to stimulate the nervous system and increase neurotransmitter production. This can help alleviate feelings of exhaustion and low energy by enhancing the body's natural ability to regulate mood and cognitive function. By supporting the brain's chemical balance, St John's Wort tinctures may help reduce fatigue and promote a sense of vitality and well-being.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue by regulating mood and reducing feelings of exhaustion.
Adhyperforin: This compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to fatigue, promoting overall physical and mental well-being.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have been found to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain and muscles, helping to increase energy levels and reduce feelings of exhaustion.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
N-feruloylserotonin: N-feruloylserotonin has been shown to exhibit antidepressant and fatigue-reducing properties, likely due to its ability to increase neurotransmitter levels and improve mood.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps reduce fatigue by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, protecting cells from damage and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Containing bioactive compounds, they are used to create tinctures that help alleviate fatigue.
Flowers: Rich in hypericins and flavonoids, they contribute to the tincture's ability to combat fatigue and improve mood.
Roots: Containing a high concentration of active compounds, they are often used in tinctures to address fatigue, anxiety, and stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried aerial parts of hypericum perforatum at dawn or early morning in a clean basket.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a clean pair of pruning scissors or a knife.
Combine 1 part of the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a clean cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to increase energy levels and reduce feelings of exhaustion. The tincture works by stimulating the adrenal glands, which produce hormones that help to combat stress and fatigue. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, which can also contribute to feelings of fatigue. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal licorice tinctures can provide natural relief from fatigue and leave you feeling more energized and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue by increasing cortisol levels in the body.
Antioxidant Properties: Licorice tincture's antioxidant properties help to protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to fatigue and other chronic health issues.

Saponin Compounds: The saponin compounds present in licorice tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce muscle soreness and joint pain that may contribute to feelings of fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to energize and revitalize the body by reducing stress and anxiety.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat physical and mental exhaustion, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra root material of sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Clean and dry the harvested root material in a warm oven or under direct sunlight for several hours.
Grind the cleaned root material into small pieces using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to enhance surface area.
Combine 1 part ground glycyrrhiza glabra root with 5 parts high-proof ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the body. This can help increase alertness and energy levels by enhancing mental clarity and focus. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties can also help reduce stress and anxiety, common underlying causes of fatigue. As a result, taking herbal lemon balm tinctures can provide a natural boost to help individuals overcome feelings of exhaustion and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Herbal lemon balm tincture helps alleviate fatigue by calming the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, which can help conserve energy.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect cells from oxidative damage and reduce chronic inflammation, which can contribute to fatigue, while its anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate joint pain and other symptoms associated with fatigue.

CNS-Stimulating Properties: The tincture contains rosmarinic acid, a natural stimulant that can help increase alertness, attention, and mental clarity, making it easier to manage fatigue and stay focused.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for tincture production to alleviate fatigue.
Seeds: The seeds of the lemon balm plant are rich in fatty acids and flavonoids, which help to promote energy and combat fatigue when used in medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their highest oil content for optimal extraction.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them thinly on paper bags or clean cloth and letting them air dry for 7-10 days.
Combine dried melissa officinalis leaves with 40% vodka or rum and cover with a lid to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much liquid as possible within 15 minutes.
Store the final melissa officinalis tincture in an amber glass bottle and keep it in a cool dark place for up to two years or refrigerate it for extended storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with fatigue because of its ability to increase alertness and boost energy levels. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a stimulating effect on the mind, helping to reduce feelings of drowsiness and mental fogginess. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate muscle tension and joint pain, common causes of fatigue. As a natural energizer, peppermint tinctures can provide a refreshing pick-me-up without relying on caffeine or other stimulants.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Peppermint tincture helps stimulate digestion, which can improve nutrient absorption and reduce feelings of fatigue caused by poor gut health.
Cooling agent: The menthol content in peppermint tincture has a cooling effect on the body, which can help increase blood flow and oxygenation to tired muscles and brain, reducing fatigue.

Adaptogenic properties: Peppermint tincture contains adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress and promote energy production, making it easier to overcome fatigue caused by mental or physical exhaustion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including fatigue, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve mental clarity and reduce fatigue by increasing oxygen flow to the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from a trusted source or local nursery.
Cleanly dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Combine 250g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with fatigue because of its natural energizing properties. The active compound, gingerol, increases blood flow and circulation, which helps to deliver oxygen and nutrients to tired cells and tissues. This can help to revitalize the body and mind, reducing feelings of exhaustion and lethargy. Additionally, ginger has a stimulating effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate metabolism and energy production. As a result, taking an herbal ginger tincture can leave you feeling refreshed, revitalized, and ready to take on your day.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Ginger helps to alleviate digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and stomach discomfort, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gut function.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fatigue by protecting cells from damage and improving overall energy metabolism.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's vasodilatory properties help to improve blood circulation, which can increase oxygen delivery to tissues and organs, reducing feelings of fatigue and increasing overall energy levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots have a long history of use in traditional medicine for its warming and energizing properties, making it an excellent candidate for treating fatigue.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the ginger plant is rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve mental clarity and reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale in the morning after the dew has dried.
Rinse the harvested ginger roots thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the active compounds to extract into the liquid.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain curcumin, a potent bioactive compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to areas of the body where inflammation may be occurring, including the brain and muscles. This can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contributes to fatigue, allowing for increased energy levels and improved mental clarity. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to increase ATP production in cells, further boosting energy levels.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by chronic fatigue syndrome, allowing for improved energy levels.

Caffeoylquinic acids (CKAs): CKAs, a class of phenolic acids present in turmeric, have been shown to possess adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to physical and mental stress, reducing feelings of fatigue and improving overall resilience.

Turmerone: Turmerone, a sesquiterpene compound found in turmeric, has been found to have stimulatory effects on the nervous system, increasing alertness, focus, and energy levels by enhancing neural activity and neurotransmitter production.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome of turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body cope with physical and mental stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh turmeric rhizomes of curcuma longa, ensuring minimal moisture content.
Dry the harvested turmeric rhizomes in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind the dried turmeric rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine 1 part turmeric powder with 2 parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar, steeping for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to nourish and balance the body's energy. The roots of the dong quai plant are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help to strengthen the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of exhaustion. Additionally, the tincture helps to regulate blood flow and oxygenation, allowing for more efficient delivery of nutrients and energy to the body's cells. As a result, dong quai tinctures can help to alleviate fatigue and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Dong Quai helps the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to fatigue by reducing inflammation and cortisol levels, allowing for improved energy production.

Vasodilatory effects: The tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels improves circulation, delivering oxygen and nutrients more efficiently to tissues and organs, helping to combat feelings of exhaustion.
Anxiolytic properties: Dong Quai has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress-related fatigue by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and polysaccharides, which help to nourish and regulate the body's energy levels.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve blood circulation and oxygenation, helping to combat fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality angelica sinensis roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily, before straining the liquid.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with date and ingredients.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place for at least 6 months or up to 2 years before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain high levels of vitamins A, C, and E, which are essential for energy production in the body. The antioxidants present in bell peppers also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to feelings of exhaustion. Furthermore, bell pepper tinctures have been shown to improve circulation and boost the immune system, both of which can help to increase energy levels and reduce fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bell peppers contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to fatigue.
Adaptogenic properties: The tannins present in bell peppers may help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of calm, reducing feelings of fatigue.
Vitamin C content: Bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which is essential for immune function and can help combat viral or bacterial infections that may contribute to fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are valued for their adaptogenic properties, helping the body cope with physical and mental exhaustion.
Seeds: The seeds of the bell pepper plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce fatigue caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried capsicum annuum peppers at peak ripeness for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested peppers to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Chop the peppers into small pieces and soak them in 400ml of vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for at least 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort tinctures helps with fatigue because it contains adaptogenic compounds that support the body's natural energy production. The tincture's active ingredients, such as centella asiatica, have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine to nourish the nervous system and boost vitality. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, Asiatic pennywort tinctures can help alleviate feelings of exhaustion and mental fogginess, allowing individuals to feel more refreshed and revitalized throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Asiatic Pennywort tincture helps to adapt the body to stressors and promotes resilience, reducing fatigue by balancing the body's response to physical and mental demands.

Antioxidant activity: The tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to fatigue and oxidative stress, protecting cells and tissues from damage and promoting energy production.

Neuroprotective effects: Asiatic Pennywort tincture has been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce mental fatigue by supporting brain health and protecting against neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and polysaccharides, which help to boost energy levels and combat fatigue by stimulating adrenal function.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve mental clarity and reduce physical exhaustion, making them ideal for tinctures targeting fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried centella asiatica leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine them with high-proof vodka or ethanol at a ratio of one part herbs to four parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to macerate for 2-4 weeks to extract active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature of around 40°f for up to 2 years.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fatigue that are treated with herbal tinctures are stress, anxiety, and insomnia. Tinctures like passionflower, lemon balm, and chamomile can help soothe an overactive mind and promote relaxation. Other tinctures like ashwagandha, rhodiola, and ginseng can boost energy levels and reduce feelings of exhaustion. These herbs can be used individually or in combination to address a range of symptoms that contribute to fatigue.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with fatigue is a blend of Ashwagandha, Ginseng, and Rhodiola. Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety, while Ginseng increases energy and vitality. Rhodiola enhances mental clarity and mood, reducing fatigue and improving overall well-being. This combination can be taken individually or blended together, and is often recommended to be taken 2-3 times a day. Consult with a healthcare professional before adding any new supplements to your routine.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy research, researchers conducted a systematic review evaluating the effectiveness and safety of herbal medicines (HMs) for cancer-related fatigue (CRF) in lung cancer patients (LCP) receiving chemotherapy. The methods used included a comprehensive search of 12 electronic medical databases for randomized controlled trials (RCTs) and quasi-RCTs investigating HMs for CRF in LCP, published up to September 2019. The results found that herbal medicines, either combined with conventional medicine or used as monotherapy, significantly improved fatigue levels, quality of life, and activities of daily life in LCP, making them a good option for alleviating fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress and promote energy balance. The tinctures support the body's natural response to fatigue by stimulating the production of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which are involved in regulating mood and energy levels. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has been shown to improve mental performance, reduce mental fatigue, and enhance overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for combating fatigue and improving energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: It helps alleviate fatigue by enhancing the body's stress response and improving mental performance.
Salidroside: It assists in reducing fatigue by acting as an antioxidant and protecting the body from oxidative stress.
Tyrosol: It helps combat fatigue by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, which regulate mood and energy levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, including rosavin and salidroside, which are responsible for the plant's adaptogenic and fatigue-fighting properties.
Leaves: They are often used in combination with other parts, such as roots and rhizomes, to create a more comprehensive tincture that can help alleviate fatigue and improve mental performance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried rhodiola rosea roots and leaves in the summer months when the plant is in bloom.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped plant material with 60-80% vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight to create a solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from the plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis tinctures helps with fatigue because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to cope with physical and mental stress. This herbal remedy is rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help protect cells from damage and promote cellular energy production. By reducing oxidative stress and supporting mitochondrial function, Schisandra chinensis tinctures can help increase energy levels and enhance endurance, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals looking to alleviate fatigue and improve overall well-being. Regular use can lead to improved vitality and resilience.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans help with fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, promoting overall well-being.
Schisandrin B: This lignan has antioxidant properties that help combat fatigue by protecting cells from damage caused by free radicals and promoting energy production.
Schisandrin A: This lignan has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate fatigue by reducing inflammation in the body and improving overall physical function.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including schisandrins, which are believed to have adaptogenic properties.
Roots: They are used because they contain a rich mixture of schisandrins and other beneficial compounds that help to mitigate fatigue and improve physical endurance.
Leaves: They are used as they contain essential oils and other nutrients that contribute to the herb's overall adaptogenic and fatigue-fighting properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried schisandra chinensis berries for tincture making.
Combine the dried berries with 750 milliliters of 50% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solids using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Filter the liquid again through a 5-micron filter and bottle the tincture for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow to the brain and muscles. This enhanced circulation enables the efficient delivery of oxygen and nutrients, which can reduce mental and physical exhaustion. Additionally, ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, contributing to a sense of energy and vitality. By promoting overall health and well-being, ginkgo biloba tinctures can help alleviate fatigue and support a more active lifestyle.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps with fatigue by improving circulation, reducing oxidative stress, and increasing the availability of oxygen to cells.
Quercetin: A flavonoid, quercetin helps with fatigue by reducing inflammation, improving blood vessel function, and enhancing the body's energy production.
Isorhapontigenin: A phenolic compound, isorhapontigenin helps with fatigue by reducing oxidative stress, improving mitochondrial function, and enhancing the body's energy metabolism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to provide the plant's medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are often used to make tinctures for fatigue because they contain ginkgolides, compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of fatigue.
Leaves: Some tinctures may also use leaves as they are rich in bilobalide, a compound that has been found to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping alleviate fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in autumn when leaves turn golden yellow.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 cup of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to extract the tincture.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eleutherococcus senticosus tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain adaptogenic compounds that support the body's natural response to stress. This ancient Asian plant, also known as Siberian ginseng, has been used for centuries to boost energy and vitality. By stimulating the body's energy reserves and enhancing its ability to adapt to physical and mental demands, Eleutherococcus senticosus tinctures help to alleviate fatigue and promote a sense of well-being, making them a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking to improve their overall energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to improved energy levels and reduced fatigue.
Eleutherosides: These constituents have adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to stress and promoting balance in physiological processes, which in turn can alleviate fatigue.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates stimulate the immune system and help to improve the body's natural energy production, reducing the feeling of fatigue and exhaustion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of eleutherosides, which are responsible for the plant's adaptogenic and fatigue-fighting properties.
Stems: They also contain eleutherosides, although in lower concentrations compared to the roots, and are often used as a complementary part for tincture production.
Barks: They contribute to the overall eleutheroside content of the tincture and may be used in combination with other parts to enhance the product's efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots and 750ml of 45 vodka for the tincture base.
Combine the dried roots with the vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the tincture to infuse.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and store the final herbal eleutherococcus senticosus tincture in a cool dark place away from children.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures helps with fatigue because it enhances mental clarity and reduces feelings of exhaustion. The herb's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, promoting balance in the body's energy systems. Ginseng tinctures also improve circulation, oxygenation, and nutrient delivery to cells, which can contribute to increased energy levels. Additionally, Panax ginseng has been shown to boost the production of ATP, the primary energy source in cells, making it a popular natural remedy for fatigue and low energy.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds help alleviate fatigue by stimulating the production of energy and reducing oxidative stress, ultimately improving physical performance and mental clarity.
Glycosides: Glycosides in ginseng tinctures help combat fatigue by acting as adaptogens, enhancing the body's ability to adapt to stress and promoting a sense of well-being.
Stigmasterol: This triterpene helps reduce fatigue by regulating the body's energy metabolism, improving circulation, and enhancing the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most commonly used part of Panax ginseng due to its high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have adaptogenic and anti-fatigue properties.
Buds: Used in some tinctures as they are believed to have a more potent and concentrated form of ginsenosides than the roots.
(Optional) Rhyzomes: Sometimes used in combination with roots as they may contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides, but their use is less common than roots and buds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-6 grams of panax ginseng root per liter of solvent in early spring or fall.
Dry the root at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Crush the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 4-6 grams of the powder with 1 liter of 70% ethanol and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures helps with fatigue because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The roots of the Valerian plant contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that interacts with the brain's GABA receptors to calm the nervous system. This leads to improved sleep quality, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall well-being. By addressing the root causes of fatigue, Valeriana officinalis tinctures offer a natural solution to help individuals regain vitality and combat exhaustion. Regular use can lead to sustained energy and mental clarity.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This compound has a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce fatigue by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Valerenal: As a potent GABA receptor agonist, valerenal helps to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress, contributing to a decrease in fatigue levels.
Isovaleric acid: This compound has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles, reducing muscle cramps and spasms, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have sedative and relaxing properties that help alleviate fatigue.
Flowers: The flowers are used to create a tincture that helps reduce anxiety and stress, contributing to a decrease in fatigue.

Barks: Although less commonly mentioned in literature, the bark of Valeriana officinalis has been used to make a tincture that may help reduce stress and fatigue due to its potential adaptogenic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of valeriana roots and stems in the spring or fall when the plant is dormant for 3 to 5 years.
Clean the roots and stems thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the valeriana roots and stems in a single layer at 40 degrees fahrenheit for 7 to 10 days.
Steep 1 part of the dried roots and stems in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka for 2 to 3 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and bottle the liquid in dark glass bottles to preserve the valeriana tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body cope with stress and promote balance in the adrenal system. Ashwagandha, the Latin name for Withania somnifera, is a natural stress-reliever that increases energy levels by reducing cortisol levels and enhancing mitochondrial function. By supporting the body's natural ability to adapt to physical and mental demands, Withania somnifera tinctures can help alleviate fatigue and promote a sense of vitality and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: Withanolides help reduce fatigue by acting as antioxidants, protecting cells from oxidative stress and promoting overall cellular health.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Withania somnifera help alleviate fatigue by modulating the body's stress response, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality.
Saponins: Saponins help combat fatigue by enhancing the body's energy production, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, making them the primary source for tincture production.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, contributing to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: They contain withanolides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and are often used in tincture formulations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the withania somnifera roots and 2 parts of the leaves and stems.
Chop the harvested withania somnifera roots and leaves into small pieces for easier infusion.
Mix 1 part of the chopped withania somnifera with 2 parts of a 95 ethanol solvent in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to collect the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius tinctures helps with fatigue because they contain ginsenosides, bioactive compounds that stimulate the production of ATP, the primary energy source in cells. By increasing ATP levels, these tinctures enhance cellular energy metabolism, reducing fatigue and exhaustion. Additionally, they promote the body's natural antioxidant defenses, protecting cells from oxidative stress and damage. This leads to improved overall energy and vitality, making it easier to manage daily tasks and activities. As a result, individuals often report a significant reduction in fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds help alleviate fatigue by enhancing mitochondrial function, increasing energy production, and promoting cellular respiration.
Saponins: Saponins in Panax quinquefolius may help reduce fatigue by modulating the body's stress response, improving immune function, and enhancing antioxidant defenses.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in the plant have been shown to help combat fatigue by reducing oxidative stress, improving blood flow to muscles, and regulating energy metabolism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to help reduce fatigue and improve mental clarity.

Stems: The stems of Panax quinquefolius are also used for tinctures, as they contain a smaller but still significant amount of ginsenosides, which help alleviate fatigue and promote overall well-being.

Leaves: The leaves of the plant are sometimes used to make tinctures, although they are less concentrated in ginsenosides than the roots and stems, but still offer potential benefits in reducing fatigue and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried panax quinquefolius roots in the fall season after the first frost has occurred.
Chop 2 cups of the harvested roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter.
Combine the chopped roots with 5 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio of one part roots to two parts solvent.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with fatigue because they promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The calming properties of lavender help to calm the mind and body, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced fatigue. Additionally, the herb's ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote a sense of balance can help alleviate fatigue caused by disrupted sleep patterns or hormonal imbalances. This natural remedy can provide a much-needed boost to those experiencing chronic fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a mild sedative and helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to fatigue.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anxiolytic and sedative properties, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue caused by emotional exhaustion.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid with antioxidant properties that helps to combat oxidative stress, a factor that can contribute to fatigue and exhaustion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain volatile oils that help to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Flowers: They are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which contribute to their calming and fatigue-reducing effects.
Stems: They also contain essential oils, which help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety associated with fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Combine the harvested flowers with 750ml of 40% ethanol in a glass container.
Seal the container and steep it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica tinctures helps with fatigue because it has been shown to improve circulation and enhance oxygen delivery to cells, reducing feelings of exhaustion and lethargy. The tincture's antioxidant properties also help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to fatigue. Additionally, Centella asiatica is believed to support the health of the nervous system, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, and may even help to regulate cortisol levels, leading to a reduction in fatigue and an increase in overall energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds help with fatigue by enhancing mitochondrial function and energy production in cells.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, contribute to fatigue relief by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Asiatic acid: Asiatic acid helps alleviate fatigue by improving cognitive function and memory, possibly due to its neuroprotective and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Centella asiatica are commonly used to make tinctures for fatigue due to their high concentration of active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids.
Barks: The barks of Centella asiatica are used for their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce fatigue and promote overall well-being.

Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica are also used to make tinctures for fatigue, as they contain a rich source of saponins and other bioactive compounds that have anti-fatigue and adaptogenic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and stems of the plant in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Steep 1 part of the chopped plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture from solids.
